---
title: "Criteria developed by those being assessed"
author: "Experiments in Assessment WG"
date: 2026-04-09
date-modified: last-modified
categories: ["Level 0", "Aim: Inclusivity", "Aim: Collaboration", "Aim: Alternative questions", "Aim: Value process", "Aim: Bias mitigation", "CoARA Commitment 2", "CoARA Commitment 6", "CoARA Commitment 8", "CoARA Commitment 10", "Target: Funder", "Target: Academic institution", "Target: Research group", "Target: Scholarly association"]
description: "Involving the applicant pool in developing the evaluation criteria for an assessment process. This can be done potentially in a phase before the call is launched, and serves to allow the applicant community to choose how they are assessed, but the evaluating body must take final responsibility for the criteria chosen."
image: false
---

::: {.callout-warning icon=false}

## Objectives and potential outcome

:::

## Research domains

## Context and considerations

Note: This idea is a core concept of the SCOPE framework. See associated idea and [SCOPE website](https://inorms.net/scope-framework-for-research-evaluation/) for more details.

## Challenges and mitigations

## Evaluating success

## Related CoARA commitments

CoARA Commitment 2 - Base research assessment primarily on qualitative evaluation for which peer review is central, supported by responsible use of quantitative indicators
CoARA Commitment 6 - Review and develop research assessment criteria, tools and processes
CoARA Commitment 8 - Exchange practices and experiences to enable mutual learning within and beyond the Coalition
CoARA Commitment 10 - Evaluate practices, criteria and tools based on solid evidence and the state-of-the-art in research on research, and make data openly available for evidence gathering and research

# Relevant resources and literature 

## Templates from funders and institutions 

## Case examples and literature

## Other resources

# Case Studies or Implementation Examples
